summary: Define bounded M7 Beta content lock contract
acceptance_criteria: Define the minimum Beta content-lock contract: scenario/person/event scope, required review states, defect-only post-lock policy, and allowed explicit balance-tuning window.
Map every M7 Roadmap and acceptance-matrix item to downstream tasks and evidence without implementing content, code, platform behavior, release artifacts, or product scope changes.
Define formal content lock acceptance as a Human Gate; this task may prepare the gate but must not approve it.
Carry forward M6_GATE = PASS_WITH_LIMITS, including Alpha scaffolding, local adviser evidence, batch tuning risk, and unsigned desktop smoke limits.
Preserve DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE exactly and state that manual node battle is only a post-1.0 non-blocking candidate unless a later Human Gate reverses it.
required_tests: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M7-CONTENT-LOCK-CONTRACT-001

target.name: M7 Beta content-lock contract v0
target.goal: Prepare a bounded Beta content-lock candidate for scenarios, persons, events, tutorial/hints/encyclopedia, audio/art/localization, balance evidence, platform evidence, save compatibility, and manual playthrough evidence without approving the formal lock.
target.content_lock_meaning: Content is lockable only after M7-CONTENT-LOCK-ACCEPTANCE-001 receives explicit Human Gate approval. Until then this task defines reviewable requirements, evidence, and stop conditions only.
target.non_map_painting_victory: Locked content must keep victory and progress centered on recognized order, vassal networks, agent politics, population/labor consequences, monsoon campaign timing, supply, succession continuity, and explainable AI, not total territorial coloring.
target.planning_only_policy: This task adds no product code, package code, content-source data, platform behavior, release artifact, roadmap edit, acceptance-matrix edit, accepted ADR edit, telemetry, server/multiplayer behavior, cloud-save implementation, arbitrary-code mod support, or manual node battle work.
target.m6_gate_carry_forward: M6_GATE = PASS_WITH_LIMITS remains in force: M6 scenario data was Alpha scaffolding, adviser evidence was local/privacy-safe, batch balance evidence records tuning risk, and Windows desktop evidence was unsigned smoke rather than release readiness.
target.manual_node_battle_policy: DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE remains exact. Manual node battle is only a post-1.0 non-blocking candidate unless a later Human Gate reverses it.
target.historical_routing_policy: Specific historical claims, names, titles, languages, symbols, music, clothing, architecture, maps, or cultural-risk decisions must route to historical_researcher before production acceptance.

scenario_person_event_scope.scenario_scope: The lock candidate covers every Beta/1.0 mainline scenario included by the accepted M7 content manifest, starting from the accepted M6 three-scenario pipeline as Alpha scaffolding rather than final content. Each scenario needs a stable ID, source/review label, starting situation, playable and non-playable polity roster, start-to-victory/failure hooks, map/content manifest reference, tutorial and encyclopedia hooks, localization keys, save/load compatibility evidence, and explicit unresolved-risk status.
scenario_person_event_scope.person_scope: The lock candidate covers every person referenced by scenarios, offices, vassal obligations, succession, diplomacy/legitimacy, adviser surfaces, events, tutorials, encyclopedia entries, art/audio/localization manifests, or save fixtures. Each person needs a stable ID, source label, review state, role-specific competencies or constraints, relationships/obligations where used, localization keys, and deterministic ordering. Persons must not become total attribute cards.
scenario_person_event_scope.event_scope: The lock candidate covers every event, policy prompt, tutorial trigger, hint, encyclopedia hook, campaign/postwar/succession/diplomacy consequence, audio/art/localization cue, and manual-playthrough evidence hook reachable from the M7 scenarios. Each event needs stable ID, trigger, choices, costs, dependencies, counterplay, source/review label, localization keys, deterministic tie-breaker, and save compatibility status.
scenario_person_event_scope.placeholder_policy: Unknown or incomplete records must be marked RESEARCH_REQUIRED, BLOCKED, DEFECT_CANDIDATE, or POST_1_0_CANDIDATE. They must not be hidden as accepted placeholders.
scenario_person_event_scope.no_fixed_culture_buff_policy: Culture, language, religion, polity, or ethnic labels may drive historically reviewed context and UI text, but they must not grant fixed mechanical buffs or penalties.

required_review_states.minimum_lock_entry: A scenario, person, or event may enter LOCK_CANDIDATE only after historical/cultural/language review where applicable, schema/content validation, runtime validation, localization coverage, save compatibility checks, balance review, platform/localization evidence, and QA review are complete or the gap is explicitly accepted as nonblocking by the correct reviewer.
required_review_states.human_gate_state: LOCKED_BY_HUMAN_GATE can be set only by M7-CONTENT-LOCK-ACCEPTANCE-001 after explicit product-owner approval. This writer task cannot set it.
required_review_states.blocked_state: CULTURE_HUMAN_GATE_REQUIRED, irreversible migration need, cloud-save implementation need, release/commercial/branding question, manual node battle reversal, or frozen architecture/platform/core change blocks autonomous acceptance.

post_lock_policy.defect_only_rule: After LOCKED_BY_HUMAN_GATE, autonomous changes are limited to blocker defect fixes, compatibility fixes, source-correction fixes, localization text corrections, accessibility/readability fixes, and the explicit balance-tuning window below.
forbidden_after_lock: arbitrary-code mods or runtime scripting extensions
manual node battle implementation or blocking research
post_lock_policy.defect_record_requirements: Every post-lock change needs a defect ID, affected content IDs, severity, reproduction or evidence, reviewer route, before/after result, save compatibility statement, and explicit statement that it is not a content expansion.
post_lock_policy.source_correction_rule: A historical or cultural correction after lock may replace an unsupported claim with a sourced, composite, fictional, or removed record, but may not expand scope without historical_researcher review and the applicable Human Gate.

balance_tuning_window.allowed_window: Explicit balance tuning may continue after formal lock and before M7 exit only through M7-LARGE-SCALE-BALANCE-ANTI-SNOWBALL-001 follow-ups or lead-created defect/tuning tasks that cite this contract.
allowed_changes: numeric parameter tuning inside existing accepted systems
AI threshold tuning using the same GameCommand surfaces as the player
scenario starting-resource corrections that preserve the accepted scenario/person/event roster
event consequence magnitude tuning without adding new event branches
localization wording needed to explain an accepted tuning change
disallowed_changes: save-format changes beyond approved compatibility fixes
balance_tuning_window.evidence_required: Each tuning proposal needs batch artifact hash, scenario/seed sample, replay pointers, before/after metrics, affected IDs, risk assessment, and gameplay_designer review before lead integration.
balance_tuning_window.window_exit: The tuning window closes at M7-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 unless a later Human Gate or explicit task extends it.

player_decisions.decision: Use vassal settlement, direct control, or restored local rule after campaign success.
player_decisions.advantage: Lets the player pursue non-map-painting victory through institutions and delegated authority.
player_decisions.cost: Creates obligations, legitimacy exposure, administrative load, or hostage/reward commitments instead of free control.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires reviewed persons, titles, obligations, postwar hooks, and save-compatible governance state.
player_decisions.counterplay: Rivals can exploit succession disputes, unpaid obligations, weak legitimacy, supply exhaustion, or local resistance.

player_decisions.decision: Mobilize labor, levies, boats, grain, or professional retainers for a campaign window.
player_decisions.advantage: Allows decisive seasonal action when routes, warehouses, and monsoon timing align.
player_decisions.cost: Consumes labor, food, cash, transport capacity, loyalty, and future harvest resilience.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires M2 population/economy/route state and M4 campaign/supply evidence in the locked scenarios.
player_decisions.counterplay: Bad timing, river/road constraints, enemy waiting, and postwar obligations can turn the mobilization into debt or collapse.

player_decisions.decision: Trust, override, or delay adviser and agent recommendations.
player_decisions.advantage: Makes agent politics useful without forcing an optimal script.
player_decisions.cost: Delays can miss monsoon windows; blind trust can entrench a faction or spend scarce resources.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires reviewed adviser/person records, reason codes, and local adviser evidence carried forward from M6 limits.
player_decisions.counterplay: Competing agents, visible reasons, and replayable outcomes let players identify bad advice without hidden global punishment.

player_decisions.decision: Pursue legitimacy, succession stability, or short-term extraction.
player_decisions.advantage: Creates alternate paths to recognized order victory and recovery from crisis.
player_decisions.cost: Legitimacy work consumes time, gifts, offices, concessions, and opportunity during campaign seasons.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires M6 diplomacy/legitimacy/succession surfaces and reviewed event/person hooks.
player_decisions.counterplay: Opponents can attack heirs, contest claims, exploit overtaxed subjects, or force campaign commitments before stabilization.

player_decisions.decision: Accept an event option involving coercion, forced movement, famine risk, enslavement, religious pressure, or wartime population loss only when the content model records human cost.
player_decisions.advantage: May produce short-term labor, cash, control, or military pressure.
player_decisions.cost: Must create explicit population, loyalty, legitimacy, relationship, and cultural-risk consequences; it cannot be a cost-free reward.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires historical_researcher review, source labels, content warnings where appropriate, and QA evidence.
player_decisions.counterplay: Subjects, rivals, vassals, heirs, and future logistics can respond through revolt risk, obligation failure, legitimacy loss, and diplomatic isolation.

runtime_state_dependencies: M2 population, labor, grain, cash, routes, season, and transport constraints
M3 offices, obligations, vassalage, postwar governance, and succession pressure
M4 campaign preparation, march, supply, automatic engagement, siege, withdrawal, and postwar handoff
M6 diplomacy, legitimacy, succession completion, policy/event/encyclopedia, layered AI, accessibility, batch balance, and adviser evidence
state.authority_rule: The lock records are content/process evidence. Authoritative world mutation remains only through the accepted simulation Worker and versioned GameCommand paths.

update_frequency.content_review: Per content record transition and per M7 review handoff; no silent bulk acceptance.
update_frequency.runtime_simulation: Existing simulation cadence remains authoritative: daily campaign/supply/march updates, scheduled economy/population updates, and event/command updates as accepted by M1-M6 systems.
update_frequency.balance_batches: Per deterministic batch artifact and replay set; no single-run final tuning.
update_frequency.manual_playthrough: Per playthrough session with build commit, seed, scenario, save checkpoints, duration, platform, locale, resolution, defects, and reviewer-visible notes.
update_frequency.gate_status: Only lead_orchestrator/Human Gate tasks may update formal gate state.

formula_intent.institutional_consequences: Advantages must be paid for through explicit ledgers such as obligations, labor, grain, cash, legitimacy, succession stability, administrative load, relationships, and campaign exposure.
formula_intent.no_hidden_global_penalty: Balance must not use invisible empire-wide tax, loyalty, weather, or AI penalties to fake institutional consequences.
formula_intent.person_model: Persons are evaluated through role fit, relationship, office, obligation, local knowledge, legitimacy, and situational constraints, not one total quality score.
formula_intent.culture_model: Culture, language, religion, polity, and lineage may change interpretation, access, risk, or reviewed content context only through explicit records. They are not fixed buffs.
formula_intent.victory_model: Victory pressure should reward durable recognized order, stable succession, credible vassal networks, and sustainable campaign logistics rather than raw territory count.
formula_intent.ai_model: AI evaluates the same explicit costs, dependencies, windows, and risks exposed to players and emits reason codes for no action, waiting, withdrawal, target changes, or settlement choices.

invariants: No content record can enter LOCK_CANDIDATE without a stable ID, deterministic ordering rule, schema validation status, review state, localization status, and source/fictionality label.
No real-world historical claim can be accepted without source registration or an explicit HISTORICAL, INFERRED, COMPOSITE, FICTIONAL, or RESEARCH_REQUIRED label.
Contested or high-risk cultural material cannot be downgraded to accepted certainty without historical_researcher review and any required Human Gate.
Forced migration, slavery, massacre, famine, religious conflict, or wartime population loss cannot be modeled as a cost-free bonus.
No content-lock or balance mechanism may grant AI-only commands or hidden player-only punishment.
Player and AI state changes remain on the same GameCommand/query/save surfaces.
Content lock acceptance remains a future Human Gate and cannot be self-approved by gameplay_designer.
DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE remains exact and cannot become an M7/M8 dependency.
M6_GATE remains PASS_WITH_LIMITS and cannot be rewritten as release readiness.
Post-lock changes must be traceable to defect IDs or explicit balance-tuning records.

ai_behavior.content_use: AI may consume locked scenario/person/event facts only through accepted read models and planning knowledge; it must not receive hidden historical certainty, free resources, or privileged action lists.
ai_behavior.balance_expectation: Large-scale batches must identify no-action-heavy AI, runaway expansion, unavoidable collapse, and direct-control dominance with replay pointers before any tuning is recommended.
ai_behavior.counterplay_visibility: Players need enough surfaced reason/evidence to understand how to counter AI timing, logistics, settlement, and legitimacy choices without exposing hidden global formulas.

ui_explanation.player_facing: UI and encyclopedia surfaces should explain concrete costs, dependencies, review-safe names, command previews, rejection reasons, AI reasons, and postwar/succession consequences through accepted read models.
ui_explanation.review_facing: Review and gate artifacts must show review states, unresolved risks, defect/tuning candidates, content manifest IDs, evidence hashes, and Human Gate blockers.
ui_explanation.no_placeholder_surface: Contract-required scenario, person, event, tutorial, hint, encyclopedia, localization, and platform surfaces must not be placeholder-only at lock candidate time.
ui_explanation.no_rule_duplication: Client UI must not duplicate authoritative sim rules; it reads accepted query/read-model surfaces.

roadmap_acceptance_mapping.evidence_required: Source records, uncertainty labels, accepted/request-change/block findings, major-risk Human Gate routing, content validation, and QA review.

roadmap_acceptance_mapping.m7_item: Full scenario, person, and event fill
roadmap_acceptance_mapping.evidence_required: Stable IDs, owner/review state, deterministic ordering, source labels, localization keys, schema/runtime validation, and explicit gap handling.
Local deterministic batch artifacts, hashes, sample sizes, replay pointers, snowball/collapse/no-action/direct-control metrics, and tuning recommendations.
Measured Beta-scale thresholds, host details, artifact hashes, before/after evidence for fixes, Web/loading/save/map/UI/desktop smoke coverage.

roadmap_acceptance_mapping.m7_item: Chrome, Edge, Firefox, Windows, resolution, and localization matrix
roadmap_acceptance_mapping.evidence_required: Browser/platform/resolution/locale pass-fail matrix, viewport/localization overlap checks, Electron security/package smoke, and unsupported-platform risk records.
M1-M6 save compatibility, M7 manifest compatibility, malformed/future/cross-manifest rejection, explicit cloud-save non-implementation boundary, and security review.
Coverage matrix for accepted systems, reviewed terminology, Storybook or Playwright evidence, viewport checks, and no placeholder-only panels.
Asset/reference manifests, localization coverage, culture-specific review records, static-resource integration checks, viewport smoke, and unresolved-risk list.
Lead synthesis of reviewed content, culture, balance, performance, platform, localization, save-boundary, and manual playthrough evidence plus explicit Human Gate approval.

roadmap_acceptance_mapping.m7_item: Beta culture review and manual playthrough
roadmap_acceptance_mapping.evidence_required: Final culture review findings, manual playthrough build/seed/scenario/save/platform/locale/resolution logs, defect list, and Human Gate routing where judgment is required.
Gate evidence document/status updates only after prerequisites and Human Gates are satisfied or explicitly blocked; no M8 release-readiness claim.

evidence_requirements.formal_lock_gate: M7-CONTENT-LOCK-ACCEPTANCE-001 must synthesize all downstream evidence and request Human Gate approval. Without approval it remains BLOCKED or PARTIAL.
evidence_requirements.handoff_rule: Each downstream task must produce a validated writer handoff and required reviewer handoff before lead integration.

parameters.name: scenario_batch_seed_count
parameters.starting_hypothesis: At least 30 deterministic seeds per locked scenario candidate are enough to detect obvious snowball/collapse/no-action patterns.
parameters.metric: runaway expansion rate, unavoidable collapse rate, no-action streak rate, direct-control dominance rate, median victory/failure year, save/hash reproducibility
parameters.validation: M7-LARGE-SCALE-BALANCE-ANTI-SNOWBALL-001 batch artifact with replay pointers and reviewer-visible hashes.

parameters.name: runaway_expansion_alert_rate
parameters.starting_hypothesis: A single polity becoming irreversible dominant in more than 15 percent of comparable runs before the intended midgame is an alert, not an automatic failure.
parameters.metric: dominance share by polity, vassal network size, direct-control share, legitimacy/succession stability, campaign supply status
parameters.validation: Batch summary plus sampled replays reviewed by balance_analyst and gameplay_designer.

parameters.name: no_action_ai_alert_rate
parameters.starting_hypothesis: AI no-action streaks above three consecutive strategic opportunities in more than 20 percent of sampled runs require reason-code review.
parameters.metric: no-action reason distribution, wait-for-season/wait-for-supply frequencies, failed target selection, blocked command reasons
parameters.validation: AI trace artifacts from large-scale balance batches and manual playthrough evidence.

parameters.name: post_lock_tuning_delta
parameters.starting_hypothesis: Post-lock tuning should prefer small numeric deltas with before/after batch evidence; any broad system or content change is a defect or Human Gate issue.
parameters.metric: affected IDs, parameter delta, before/after metric movement, regression count, save compatibility result
parameters.validation: Defect/tuning register entry, deterministic tests, and reviewer handoff.

parameters.name: manual_playthrough_minimum
parameters.starting_hypothesis: One complete manual Beta playthrough per locked scenario candidate plus targeted regression passes can supply evidence for content lock recommendation, but human acceptance remains a gate.
parameters.metric: completion status, blocker defects, confusion points, save checkpoints, platform/locale/resolution coverage, post-lock tuning candidates
parameters.validation: M7-MANUAL-PLAYTHROUGH-EVIDENCE-001 evidence handoff and QA review.

boundaries: This contract prepares content lock only; it does not approve content lock.
No formal historical content is created by this task.

No product code, package code, content-source data, tooling, CI, roadmap, acceptance matrix, ADR, prior gate, release artifact, telemetry, server/multiplayer, cloud-save implementation, arbitrary-code mod support, or manual node battle work is authorized here.

Formal content lock acceptance, major cultural-risk decisions, manual playthrough acceptance where judgment is required, cloud-save implementation, irreversible migration, release/commercial/branding decisions, telemetry, server/multiplayer, arbitrary-code mods, frozen architecture/platform/core changes, manual node battle reversal, and formal 1.0 scope reversal remain Human Gates.
M7 content lock must not claim M8 release security/SBOM/install/update/rollback readiness.

exploits.exploit: Post-lock content creep disguised as a defect fix.
exploits.countermeasure: Require defect ID, affected IDs, before/after evidence, reviewer route, and explicit no-expansion statement.

exploits.exploit: Balance by hidden global punishment instead of institutional consequence.
exploits.countermeasure: Require explicit ledgers and visible causes for obligations, labor, grain, legitimacy, succession, route, and supply consequences.

exploits.exploit: Culture or ethnicity min-maxing through fixed buffs.
exploits.countermeasure: Reject fixed culture/ethnicity buffs; use reviewed context-specific records and costs only.

exploits.exploit: Total-character-score sorting makes persons generic stat cards.
exploits.countermeasure: Require role fit, relationships, obligations, legitimacy, office, and situational constraints instead of one aggregate score.

exploits.exploit: AI-only escape routes hide content or balance failures.
exploits.countermeasure: Require same GameCommand surface, reason traces, and replayable evidence for AI decisions.

exploits.exploit: Manual node battle returns as an M7 blocker through playthrough feedback.
exploits.countermeasure: Preserve DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE exactly; record only as post-1.0 non-blocking candidate unless later Human Gate reverses it.

exploits.exploit: Unsupported historical certainty slips through localization or art/audio assets.
exploits.countermeasure: Route real-world details to historical_researcher, require source labels, and block major cultural risk for Human Gate.

exploits.exploit: Cloud-save or telemetry is added as quality evidence infrastructure.
exploits.countermeasure: Keep evidence local and reviewable; cloud-save implementation, accounts, secrets, telemetry, and remote collection remain Human Gates.

vertical_slice_acceptance: The contract reaches REVIEW with gameplay_designer as writer and systems_architect as reviewer; gameplay_designer does not approve its own design implementation.
M7-HISTORICAL-CULTURAL-LANGUAGE-REVIEW-001 receives this contract before formal content fill and routes real historical/cultural details through historical_researcher.
M7-SCENARIO-PERSON-EVENT-FILL-001 fills only the contracted scenario/person/event scope through explicit schemas, source labels, stable IDs, and deterministic ordering.
M7-LARGE-SCALE-BALANCE-ANTI-SNOWBALL-001 produces local deterministic evidence before any post-lock tuning recommendation.
M7-CONTENT-LOCK-ACCEPTANCE-001 treats formal content lock as a Human Gate and remains BLOCKED/PARTIAL without explicit product-owner approval.
M7-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 validates M7 content and quality evidence without claiming M8 release readiness.

roadmap_and_matrix_items_mapped: save migration and cloud-save candidate boundaries
acceptance_evidence.advantage_cost_dependency_counterplay_ch: Every player_decisions entry includes advantage, cost, dependencies, and counterplay.
acceptance_evidence.formal_content_lock_human_gate: Prepared only; not approved by this task.
acceptance_evidence.m6_gate_carry_forward: M6_GATE = PASS_WITH_LIMITS with Alpha scaffolding, local adviser evidence, batch tuning risk, and unsigned desktop smoke limits preserved.
acceptance_evidence.manual_node_battle_allowed_mention: post-1.0 non-blocking candidate only unless a later Human Gate reverses it

gameplay_designer.next_action: Await systems_architect review of the planning-only content lock contract.

notes: First executable downstream M7 task after the task graph is accepted; planning-only contract work.
M6_GATE remains PASS_WITH_LIMITS and must not be rewritten as unconditional PASS or release readiness.
Manual node battle remains DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE; this task must not create implementation, M7/M8 blocking spike, research, validation, or exit-gate dependency for manual node battle.
Player and AI behavior must continue through the same GameCommand/query/save surfaces where implementation work is later authorized.
sim-core must remain decoupled from React, Pixi, Electron, DOM, Node IO, real time APIs, hidden RPC, and object-graph serialization.
Stop and route to the named reviewer or Human Gate if allowed paths are insufficient, required tests cannot run, or a Human Gate decision is needed.
human_gates: manual playthrough acceptance where human judgment is required
commercial, release, store, branding, logo, or trademark decisions
telemetry, analytics, remote collection, accounts, paid services, or secrets
arbitrary-code mods, runtime scripting extensions, or Workshop support
cloud-save implementation, network persistence, or any account-backed save service
frozen product, platform, core architecture, technology stack, simulation authority, deterministic, or historical-policy changes
manual node battle reversal or any formal 1.0 scope reversal
